events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need return exactly one valid JSON object matching schema. Need analyze scenario: timing marks misaligned by a tooth (photo of marks - ME/ID). Work rough-complete. Trade automotive powertrain. Reference OEM service info. Need estimate measurement and judge whether within tolerance. Timing marks misaligned by a tooth is clearly out of tolerance for engine timing. Decision fail, risk high or critical? Misaligned by a tooth can cause valve-piston interference, engine damage, performance issues, potential catastrophic failure. Risk maybe critical if engine could be destroyed, but in production floor before start maybe high. The schema risk options: low medium high critical. Timing misalignment by tooth is critical? Could lead to engine failure, severe damage. For quality gate, often critical. Component conditions installed-defective and non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 70? Rough-complete. Value maybe 1 tooth misalignment. Confidence high 0.95. event_time, rate, order, workable not audio not needed default empty list? Schema includes those fields; we can set event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order [], workable [], sound_source "". findings: timing marks misaligned by one tooth; cam/crank timing out of spec; potential valve timing error. actions: remove/realign timing components per OEM service info; verify timing marks; re-torque; re-inspect; document. rationale: OEM timing tolerances require precise alignment of timing marks; a one-tooth error exceeds allowable tolerance and can cause valve timing error, poor performance, or mechanical interference. references: OEM service info. Need valid JSON. No prose. Ensure raw JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 1,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Timing marks are misaligned by one tooth",
    "Cam/crank timing is outside OEM specification",
    "Risk of valve timing error and mechanical interference"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove and realign timing components per OEM service info",
    "Verify timing marks align exactly at TDC / specified position",
    "Re-torque fasteners to spec",
    "Re-inspect timing alignment before closing out",
    "Document corrective action and reinspection results"
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM timing tolerances require exact alignment of timing marks; a one-tooth misalignment exceeds allowable tolerance and can cause incorrect valve timing, reduced performance, or valve-to-piston contact.",
  "references": ["OEM service info"]
}
